title: vouacapoua inermis
intro: Vouacapoua inermis, also known as the West African pouched corkwood, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Africa. The bark of the Vouacapoua inermis plant is traditionally used in medicine, rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Vouacapoua inermis have been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the bark of Vouacapoua inermis is often dried and crushed to create a powder, which can be used in teas, infusions, or capsules.
